records.ety: [OE. test test, or cupel, potsherd, F. tt, from L. testum an earthen vessel; akin to testa a piece of burned clay, an earthen pot, a potsherd, perhaps for tersta, and akin to torrere to patch, terra earth (cf. Thirst, and Terrace), but cf. Zend tasta cup. Cf. Test a shell, Testaceous, Tester a covering, a coin, Testy, Tte--tte.]
usage: -- Test, Trial. Trial is the wider term; test is a searching and decisive trial. It is derived from the Latin testa (earthen pot), which term was early applied to the fining pot, or crucible, in which metals are melted for trial and refinement. Hence the peculiar force of the word, as indicating a trial or criterion of the most decisive kind.

defs: A cupel or cupelling hearth in which precious metals are melted for trial and refinement.
quotes: Our ingots, tests, and many mo.

defs: Examination or trial by the cupel; hence, any critical examination or decisive trial; as, to put a man's assertions to a test.
Means of trial; as, absence is a test of love.
quotes: Each test every light her muse will bear.

defs: That with which anything is compared for proof of its genuineness; a touchstone; a standard.
quotes: Life, force, and beauty must to all impart, At once the source, and end, and test of art.

defs: Discriminative characteristic; standard of judgment; ground of admission or exclusion.
quotes: Our test excludes your tribe from benefit.
Who would excel, when few can make a test Betwixt indifferent writing and the best?

defs: A reaction employed to recognize or distinguish any particular substance or constituent of a compound, as the production of some characteristic precipitate; also, the reagent employed to produce such reaction; thus, the ordinary test for sulphuric acid is the production of a white insoluble precipitate of barium sulphate by means of some soluble barium salt.

A set of questions to be answered or problems to be solved, used as a means to measure a person's knowledge, aptitude, skill, intelligence, etc.; in school settings, synonymous with examination or exam; as, an intelligence test. Also used attributively; as a test score, test results.
quotes: I leave him to your gracious acceptance, whose trial shall better publish his commediation.
Thy virtue, prince, has stood the test of fortune, Like purest gold, that tortured in the furnace, Comes out more bright, and brings forth all its weight.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tested; p. pr. & vb. n. Testing.]

defs: To refine, as gold or silver, in a test, or cupel; to subject to cupellation.

To put to the proof; to prove the truth, genuineness, or quality of by experiment, or by some principle or standard; to try; as, to test the soundness of a principle; to test the validity of an argument.
quotes: Experience is the surest standard by which to test the real tendency of the existing constitution.

defs: To examine or try, as by the use of some reagent; as, to test a solution by litmus paper.

To administer a test{8} to (someone) for the purpose of ascertaining a person's knowledge or skill; especially, in academic settings, to determine how well a student has learned the subject matter of a course of instruction.

quotes: Prelates and great lords of England, who were for the more surety tests of that deed.

defs: To make a testament, or will.
